newsletters.subject: [AINews] The End of Finetuning
newsletters.rationale: OpenAI deprecating finetuning APIs — major industry shift. swyx argues finetuning is a casualty of the 2026 side quest massacre, referencing Jeremy Howard's 2023 prediction that most use cases don't need finetuning. Directly impacts MLOps/Training wiki pages (TRL, Axolotl, PEFT). Also covers Anthropic's valuation surpassing OpenAI for the first time.
suggested_actions: Update wiki/concepts/finetuning.md with deprecation context
Create/更新 wiki/events/openai-finetuning-deprecation.md
Cross-reference with wiki concepts on context engineering vs finetuning
Link to Jeremy Howard 2023 podcast episode reference
Update wiki/entities/swyx.md with latest Latent Space coverage

newsletters.subject: Agentic Engineering and the Lost Art of Verification
newsletters.rationale: Features Wes McKinney (pandas creator), Jeremiah Lowin (Prefect), and Randy Olson (Good Eye Labs) discussing their actual agent workflows. Key themes: RoboRev background code reviewer, skills as thin drivers, progressive disclosure, managing context rot, ephemeral 'just for me' software. Directly relevant to autonomous-ai-agents and coding agent wiki.
suggested_actions: Update wiki/entities/wes-mckinney.md with agent workflow insights
Create/更新 wiki/concepts/agentic-engineering.md
Update wiki/concepts/context-engineering.md with progressive disclosure patterns
Link to Superpowers skills framework mentioned by Wes McKinney
Add to wiki/entities/jeremiah-lowin.md and wiki/entities/randy-olson.md

newsletters.subject: Learn the system
newsletters.rationale: Ben Tossell reacts to 'Agentic coding is a trap — and we all fell for it' video. Discusses live models making a comeback, AI infrastructure memory bottlenecks (Coatue analysis on 5x memory demand in 5 years). Also covers Lightfield AI-native CRM with Skills feature, AGENTS.md patterns.
suggested_actions: Update wiki/concepts/agentic-coding.md with 'trap' debate perspective
Cross-reference with Coatue memory infrastructure analysis
Link to AGENTS.md pattern discussion
Update wiki/entities/ben-tossell.md with latest coverage

newsletters.subject: How open model ecosystems compound
newsletters.rationale: Nathan Lambert (ex-Meta, DeepMind, HuggingFace) discusses open model ecosystem funding sustainability. Key thesis: in 5-10 years, as models get more expensive and capable, funding structures for open models may break down. Relates to broader open vs closed AI dynamics, talent concentration in robotics/physical AI, and whether open-source can democratize access to physical AI capability like it did for LLMs.
suggested_actions: Update wiki/entities/nathan-lambert.md with latest thesis
Create/更新 wiki/concepts/open-model-ecosystems.md
Cross-reference with Bessement robotics predictions on open vs closed dynamics
Link to LeRobot, Genesis, Isaac Lab open-source ecosystem analysis

newsletters.subject: I Ran 75 Tests on Claude Skills. Here's What Broke.
newsletters.rationale: Aakash Gupta shares results from 75+ Claude Skills tests over 6 months of daily use. Claims Skills are 'highest alpha technique in AI right now.' Covers skill design patterns, what works, what breaks. Directly relevant to Hermes skills architecture and the broader Claude Skills ecosystem.
suggested_actions: Update wiki/concepts/claude-skills.md with 7-law framework
Cross-reference with Hermes agent skills design patterns
Compare with Ruben Hassid's 'Claude Skills' coverage and Ilia Karelin's Skills Playbook
Update wiki/entities/aakash-gupta.md

newsletters.subject: The End of Turn-Based Voice-AI
newsletters.rationale: Discusses evolution beyond turn-based voice AI architectures. The Beehiiv link structure suggests this is from an infrastructure/voice AI focused newsletter. Topic is adjacent to core LLM/Agent wiki scope but relevant for multimodal AI coverage. Less critical than other items this batch.

Agentic engineering workflows — real-world patterns from pandas/Prefect creators
